div1.entryFree: Complot, subst. (cómplot and complót), plot, concerted plan: their c. is to have my life, H6B III, 1, 147. Lord Hastings will not yield to our --s, R3 III, 1, 192. we may digest our --s in some form, R3 III, 1, 192 this fatal writ, the c. of this timeless tragedy, Tit. II, 3, 265. --s of mischief, V, 1, 65. Revenge now goes to lay a c. to betray thy foes, V, 2, 147.
